4# IMPIN — LLM Context File

> This file provides structured context about the IMPIN project for use by
> large language models, AI coding assistants, and automated agents.

---

## What is IMPIN?

IMPIN (Informal Market Price Intelligence Network) is an academic data science
capstone project at Africa Business School (UM6P), Rabat, Morocco.

**Academic Claim:**
> *IMPIN is a real-time food price intelligence system for Ghana with three layers:*
> *(1) a live scrape-based price index updated weekly;*
> *(2) a nowcast of the current food CPI component before official GSS release;*
> *(3) a 1–3 month forecast comparing classical time-series and ML models.*

**Students:** Acheampong Yaw HINNEH · Abiola OKUNSANYA  
**Year:** 2025–2027 · **Pilot city:** Accra, Ghana

---

## System Overview

The system has two data tracks that feed a unified dashboard:

**Track 1 — Historical Forecasting (WFP data, 48 months):**
- Data: WFP VAM Ghana food prices + GHS/USD rate + Brent crude (FRED)
- Panel: `data/processed/historical_panel.parquet` — 48 months, aligned monthly
- Models: ARIMAX(1,1,0) [done, 83.3% directional accuracy] + XGBoost + RF + Prophet
- Purpose: Forecast food price index 1–3 months ahead; compare models

**Track 2 — Live Cross-Section (scraped data, 1 snapshot):**
- Data: `data/raw/scraped_combined.csv` — 1,586 products, May 2026, 5 sources
- Models: Isolation Forest + K-Means + Z-score
- Purpose: Detect anomalous prices, identify expensive clusters

**Glue (Nowcast):**
- Append IMPIN snapshot as latest point in historical series
- Run best forecasting model forward to current period
- Compare: model estimate for May 2026 vs. IMPIN live snapshot
- This validates the 3-layer claim

---

## Current Status (May 2026)

| Component | Status |
|---|---|
| All scrapers | ✅ Done |
| scraped_combined.csv | ✅ 1,586 products |
| WFP historical panel | ✅ 48 months |
| EDA (15 plots + PDF) | ✅ Done |
| Stationarity (all I(1)) | ✅ Done |
| Granger VAR(1) | ✅ Done (null result, publishable) |
| ARIMAX(1,1,0) | ✅ Done (83.3% dir acc) |
| XGBoost + RF | 🔜 Next |
| Prophet | 🔜 Next |
| Nowcast validation | 🔜 Pending |
| Anomaly detection | 🔜 Pending |
| Streamlit dashboard | 🔜 Pending |

---

## Domain Vocabulary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| IMPIN | The full system (this project) |
| wfp_food_index | WFP food price index constructed from VAM Ghana data, base 2019-08=100 |
| CPI | Consumer Price Index — official measure published by Ghana Statistical Service (GSS) |
| Nowcasting | Estimating the current value of an economic variable before official data is published |
| VAR | Vector Autoregression — multivariate time-series model used for Granger testing |
| ADF | Augmented Dickey-Fuller test — stationarity check |
| I(1) | Integrated of order 1 — stationary after first differencing |
| GSS | Ghana Statistical Service — official CPI publisher |
| WFP VAM | World Food Programme Vulnerability Analysis and Mapping — our historical data source |
| base_period | January 2026 = 100 — IMPIN snapshot normalisation reference |
| RANDOM_SEED | 42 — used in all stochastic model components |

---

## Tech Stack

- **Python:** 3.14
- **Scraping:** Requests, BeautifulSoup, Playwright (JS-rendered sites)
- **Data:** pandas, numpy, Parquet (processed), CSV (raw)
- **Statistics:** statsmodels 0.14.6 (VAR, ARIMAX, ADF, KPSS), scipy
- **ML:** scikit-learn — XGBoost (via sklearn API), Random Forest, Isolation Forest, K-Means
- **Forecasting:** Prophet (Meta), statsmodels
- **Visualisation:** matplotlib (Agg backend), Plotly
- **Dashboard:** Streamlit

---

## Data Sources (confirmed)

| Source | Status | Role |
|---|---|---|
| WFP VAM Ghana | ✅ Live | 14 commodities × 48 months historical |
| Brent crude (FRED) | ✅ Live | Exogenous predictor |
| GHS/USD rate | ✅ Implied from WFP | Exchange rate proxy |
| FAO Food Price Index | ❌ Dropped | API unreachable |
| GSS Ghana CPI | ❌ Dropped | PDF-only, no scrape path |
| Konzoom (Shopify) | ✅ Live | 708 products |
| Makola Stores (Shopify) | ✅ Live | 580 products |
| Big Samps Market (WooCommerce) | ✅ Live | 214 products |
| Shopnaw | ✅ Live | 74 products |
| KiKUU | ✅ Live | 10 products |

---

## Constraints for Code Generation

- Python 3.14 at `C:\Users\ayhin\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\`
- matplotlib backend: always `matplotlib.use('Agg')` before import
- Random seed: `RANDOM_SEED = 42`
- Train/test split: train ≤ 2022-06, test 2022-07 → 2023-07
- ML feature set: lag-1/2/3 of target, log_ghsusd, brent_lag6, month dummies
- No LSTM/PyTorch — out of scope
- No Granger causality as "primary finding" — it is a null result with interpretable context
- No PII. No credentials in code.

---

## File Layout

```
IMPIN/
├── scrapers/           # run_all.py + per-source scrapers
├── pipeline/           # build_historical.py, build_index.py
├── models/             # arimax_model.py, ml_forecast.py, prophet_model.py
│   └── results/        # arimax_comparison.csv, comparison.csv
├── analysis/           # eda.py, stationarity.py, granger.py
├── anomaly/            # detector.py
├── app/                # dashboard.py
├── data/
│   ├── raw/            # scraped_combined.csv (gitignored)
│   ├── processed/      # historical_panel.parquet (gitignored)
│   └── external/       # wfp_ghana_monthly_national.parquet, proxy_series.parquet
├── outputs/
│   ├── plots/          # 01_*.png … 10c_*.png
│   └── *.csv           # reports
└── config/
    └── cpi_basket.yaml
```

---

## Academic References

- Cavallo (2018). Scraped Data and Sticky Prices. *REStat* 100(1), 105–119.
- Cavallo & Rigobon (2016). The Billion Prices Project. *JEP* 30(2), 151–178.
- WFP VAM (2023). Food Security and Market Monitoring in West Africa.
- IMF WP/22/88 (2022). Overcoming Data Sparsity in Sub-Saharan Africa.
